one_line_thesis: AEP is a large regulated utility investing in transmission, distribution, and generation across eleven states.

deep_dive.executive_summary: AEP is a large regulated utility investing in transmission, distribution, and generation across eleven states. FY2025 diluted earnings were $6.66 per share on $21.9 billion of revenue; the annual dividend is $3.74 per share. At $131.05, Marvin's base case implies 7.3% per year over seven years on normalized regulated earnings power. The moat is narrow and dhando is partial: allowed-return frameworks cap catastrophic downside, but $47.5 billion of net debt and planned equity issuance bound the upside. Stance proposal: watch. No approved third-party sources are in base IRR.

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
applicability_tests: Incremental capital can be tied to identifiable projects, cohorts, or contracted capacity.
Returns on new capital can be separated from returns on the installed base.
Contract duration, volume protection, pricing, and counterparty risk are observable.
Leverage and future owner-funded capital are explicitly deducted.
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K: stockholders' equity $31.1B; economic value uses normalized owner cash ($6.4/sh), not GAAP book alone.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of AEP, including regulated owner-cash engine, data-center load option, net financial claims, and regulatory execution reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Regulated engine valued through owner-cash discount on normalized FY2025 earnings; net debt and regulatory reserve are separate overlap keys.

personas.falsifier: FCF yield < 0.5% or commoditized archetype (turnaround/optionality only)
personas.label: Chris Hohn / TCI

personas.falsifier: dhando -> none OR net-debt/EBITDA > 3 OR bear return < -15%
personas.label: Mohnish Pabrai

personas.falsifier: No asset intensity / pricing power (moat eroding + non-croupier)
personas.label: Murray Stahl

personas.falsifier: FCF yield below universe median OR base IRR below median
personas.label: Joel Greenblatt

personas.falsifier: Operating lens only — no dated inefficiency / catalyst
personas.label: John Mihaljevic / MOI

personas.falsifier: No dated contractual payoff or royalty/trust structure
personas.label: Horizon Kinetics

personas.falsifier: Supply response, reinvestment requirements, or incremental returns cannot be measured.
personas.label: Marathon / Capital Returns

personas.falsifier: Debt maturities, refinancing exposure, liquidity, or downside recovery cannot be reconciled.
personas.label: Howard Marks / Oaktree

personas.falsifier: Asset value lacks an independently evidenced realization path or is already capitalized in operating cash flow.
personas.label: Klarman / Baupost

bullets.title: Phorcys · discussed · AEP · 2021Q2
bullets.summary: e payments in order to “preserve liquidity” – a tactic it had used just a few months earlier on its larger portfolio, the American Eagle Portfolio (“AEP”).

timeline.label: FY2025 results: revenue $21.9B (+15%); operating income $5.3B; diluted EPS $6.66; $72B 2026-2030 capital plan disclosed
FY2025 dividend declared $3.74/sh; approximately $5.6B equity issuance planned to fund capital plan; $3.5B ATM program available
Management pivots capital plan toward transmission upgrades and new generation to serve data-center and large-load demand across 11-state footprint
Ongoing state rate cases and FERC transmission formula rates govern allowed ROE on growing rate base
Accelerated transmission investment in PJM and ERCOT footprints; Transource joint ventures for regional lines
Coal retirement and renewable additions continue under integrated resource plans across operating companies
AEP remains a publicly traded holding company for vertically integrated and T&D operating utilities in 11 states

industry.structure: Regulated electric utilities operate geographic monopolies with returns set by state commissions and FERC on transmission; AEP is among the largest US wires and generation owners with ~$72B five-year capital plan.
industry.share_shift: Load growth from data centers and industrial electrification favors transmission-heavy utilities with available interconnection capacity; AEP competes for large-load customers with peers DUK, SO, and EXC in overlapping regions.
industry.trend: Affordability pressure on customer bills, rising interest rates on utility debt, and equity issuance to fund capex are the dominant industry themes; data-center demand is the primary growth tailwind for wires owners.
